title: ICE Detainee Deaths Reach 18 in 2026 — On Track to Surpass All-Time Record
excerpt: The death of 33-year-old Cuban national Denny Adan Gonzalez at the Stewart Detention Center in Georgia marked the 18th reported ICE custody death of 2026, bringing the annual total to a pace that would shatter the all-time record. CBS News analysis found 2025 already had the highest death rate per detainee since COVID-19, and advocacy groups said 2026 was on pace to exceed even that.
content:
On approximately May 1, 2026, ICE reported the death of Denny Adan Gonzalez, a 33-year-old Cuban national, at the Stewart Detention Center in Lumpkin, Georgia. The Detention Watch Network reported this was the 18th death in ICE custody in just four months of 2026.

  • Gonzalez was found unresponsive in his cell at approximately 10:25 p.m. ICE described the suspected cause of death as suicide, though the official cause remained under investigation.
  • Immigrant rights groups described the Stewart Detention Center as one of the deadliest ICE facilities in the country, citing alleged inhumane conditions, inadequate mental health care, and heavy use of solitary confinement. Attorneys demanded an independent investigation.
  • According to research organization KFF, at least 46 people had died while in ICE custody since January 2025 — across both 2025 and the first months of 2026.
  • CBS News analysis found that even before 2026, 2025 had the highest ICE detention death rate — 5.6 people per 10,000 detainees — since the COVID-19 pandemic. The 2026 pace suggested that record would be broken.
  • ICE's detention population had soared to more than 70,000 people earlier in 2026, higher than under any prior administration, before scaling back somewhat following the Minneapolis protests.
  • As of May 27, 2026, Wikipedia's list of ICE detainee deaths recorded 19 publicized deaths, with the true total potentially higher due to ICE's practice of releasing individuals shortly before death to avoid counting them in official statistics.
"I have never seen anything like this, where I'm seeing ICE reporting out at least one death per week," said Setareh Ghandehari, advocacy director at Detention Watch Network.
source: CBS News / WUNC / Detention Watch Network
mediaPlaceholder: CBS News analysis chart showing ICE detainee death rate per 10,000 detainees from 2018 through 2026
imageAlt: ICE Detainee Deaths Reach 18 in 2026 — On Track to Surpass All-Time Record
